questions.question: What is the primary function of the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: The atmosphere provides oxygen for respiration and protects living organisms from harmful radiation.

questions.question: Which layer of the atmosphere extends up to 600 km?
questions.explanation: The troposphere is the lowest layer of the atmosphere, extending up to about 6-15 km above the Earth's surface.

questions.question: What is the process by which water turns into vapour and rises?
questions.explanation: Evaporation occurs when water molecules gain energy from their surroundings and change state from liquid to gas.

questions.question: What is the process by which water vapour cools and forms clouds?
questions.explanation: Condensation occurs when water vapour in the air cools and changes state from gas to liquid, forming droplets of water.

questions.question: What is the process by which plants release water vapour into the air?
questions.explanation: Transpiration occurs when plants release water vapour into the air through their leaves.

questions.question: What is the process by which water falls to the ground as rain, snow, sleet, or hail?
questions.explanation: Precipitation occurs when water vapour in the air condenses and falls to the ground.

questions.question: What is the process by which phosphorus comes from rocks into soil?
questions.explanation: Weathering occurs when rocks are broken down through physical or chemical means, releasing phosphorus into the soil.

questions.question: What is the process by which plants take phosphorus from soil?
questions.explanation: Plant absorption occurs when plants absorb phosphorus from the soil through their roots.

questions.question: What is the process by which dead organisms return phosphorus to soil?
questions.explanation: Decomposition occurs when dead organisms are broken down by microorganisms, releasing phosphorus back into the soil.

questions.question: What is the process by which phosphorus is washed into rivers and oceans?
questions.explanation: Runoff occurs when excess water from rainfall flows over the land, carrying phosphorus with it into nearby waterways.

questions.question: What is the main contributor to global warming?
questions.correct_answer: Increased concentrations of carbon dioxide (CO₂), methane (CH₄), and other greenhouse gases from industrial activities, deforestation, and agriculture
questions.explanation: The increased concentration of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere traps heat, leading to global warming.

notes.title: Atmospheric Layers
notes.content: The Earth's atmosphere is divided into five main layers: troposphere, stratosphere, mesosphere, thermosphere, and exosphere.

notes.title: Evaporation Process
notes.content: Evaporation occurs when water molecules gain energy from their surroundings and change state from liquid to gas.

notes.title: Condensation Process
notes.content: Condensation occurs when water vapour in the air cools and changes state from gas to liquid, forming droplets of water.

notes.title: Transpiration Process
notes.content: Transpiration occurs when plants release water vapour into the air through their leaves.

notes.title: Precipitation Process
notes.content: Precipitation occurs when water vapour in the air condenses and falls to the ground.

facts.fact: The atmosphere is made up of about 78% nitrogen, 21% oxygen, and 1% other gases.
The troposphere extends up to about 6-15 km above the Earth's surface.
Evaporation occurs when water molecules gain energy from their surroundings and change state from liquid to gas.
Condensation occurs when water vapour in the air cools and changes state from gas to liquid, forming droplets of water.
Transpiration occurs when plants release water vapour into the air through their leaves.
